title: Is Apple-pie Gluten Free? | BiteRight
description: Public gluten safety analysis for Apple-pie. See major risks, safer alternatives, and what to ask before ordering.
heading: Is Apple-pie gluten free?
intro: This public analysis report explains the biggest gluten risks in Apple-pie and how to order more safely.

verdict.summary: Apple pie typically contains wheat flour in the crust, which poses a significant gluten risk for individuals with coeliac disease. Cross-contamination during preparation can also be a concern.

disclaimer: This guidance is informational only. Always verify ingredients and preparation with the restaurant.

sections.title: Quick answer
sections.body: Apple-pie can vary by recipe, ingredients, and cross-contact controls in the kitchen.

sections.title: Common gluten risks
sections.body: Watch for hidden sources like soy sauce, malt flavoring, marinades, thickeners, and shared fryers.

sections.title: How BiteRight helps
sections.body: Scan menus and ingredient labels with BiteRight to get a localized gluten-risk breakdown in seconds.

risk: Pre-made pie crusts (if not labeled gluten-free)

waiter_script.preview: Is the apple pie made with a gluten-free crust and prepared in a dedicated gluten-free area?

faq.question: Can BiteRight confirm if Apple-pie is gluten free?
faq.answer: BiteRight highlights likely gluten risks based on ingredients and preparation. Always confirm with the kitchen if you have coeliac disease.

faq.question: What should I ask a restaurant?
faq.answer: Ask about shared equipment, sauces, marinades, and whether the kitchen has a dedicated gluten-free prep area.
